title: Angelini Ventures Marks First Portfolio Company Exit as ResMed Acquires Noctrix Health for $340 million
author: Not Specified
description: ResMed has acquired Noctrix Health, a medical technology company specializing in wearable neuromodulation therapies for sleep and neurological conditions, for $340 million. This marks the first successful exit for Angelini Ventures, a corporate venture firm that invested in Noctrix during its $40 million Series C round in 2024. The acquisition will integrate Noctrix's FDA-authorized Nidra therapy for Restless Legs Syndrome into ResMed's broader sleep health ecosystem.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: ResMed is acquiring an innovative FDA-approved medical technology company that addresses unmet medical needs in sleep health. The acquisition strengthens ResMed's position in the sleep health market and expands its connected health ecosystem with a differentiated first-in-class therapy.

title: 9 Stocks That Could Defy the ’Sell in May and Go Away’ Trend This Time
author: David Wagner
description: Despite the traditional 'Sell in May and Go Away' market pattern, 2026 shows strong potential for continued stock gains. With 84% of companies beating earnings expectations and 27% earnings growth, selective investing in fundamentally strong companies could outperform. The article highlights two healthcare stocks—DexCom and ResMed—as well-positioned to deliver significant upside potential despite trading below analyst price targets.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q1 2026 results with EPS beating estimates ($0.56 vs $0.47), 15% YoY revenue growth, raised operating margin outlook, and stock trading below average analyst price target of $86.88 with 30%+ upside potential

Q3 fiscal 2026 results exceeded expectations with EPS of $2.86 vs $2.79 estimate, 10.8% YoY revenue growth, expanded gross margins to 62.2%, and Royal Bank of Canada raised price target to $321 with outperform rating

title: Sleep Apnea Devices (Therapeutic, Diagnostic) Market Report 2026-2034 Featuring Key Players - ResMed, Koninklijke Philips, and Carefusion
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global sleep apnea devices market is expected to grow at a CAGR of 7.05% from 2025 to 2034, driven by rising prevalence of sleep apnea affecting approximately 1 billion people worldwide, increased obesity rates, and technological innovations in CPAP, BiPAP, and oral appliances. Growth is further supported by expanding homecare and telemedicine adoption. However, challenges include high device costs, limited access in developing regions, and poor patient compliance with therapy.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: ResMed is identified as a key player in the expanding sleep apnea devices market, which is projected to grow at 7.05% CAGR through 2034. The company benefits from increasing prevalence of sleep apnea, technological advancements, and growing homecare adoption trends.

Philips is listed as a major player in the sleep apnea devices market during a period of strong projected growth. The company stands to benefit from rising awareness of sleep disorders and continuous innovation in device technology.

title: Global Respiratory Care Devices Market to Grow at a Substantial Growth Rate of ~8% by 2034 | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: The global respiratory care devices market is projected to grow from USD 24.3 billion in 2025 to USD 47.5 billion by 2034, at a CAGR of ~8%. Growth is driven by rising prevalence of chronic respiratory diseases (COPD, asthma, sleep apnea), increasing demand for home healthcare solutions, technological advancements, and post-COVID-19 awareness. North America leads with 41% market share, followed by Europe and Asia-Pacific.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Identified as a leading player in the expanding respiratory care devices market, well-positioned to capture growth from rising chronic respiratory disease prevalence and home healthcare demand.
Key company in the respiratory care devices market experiencing substantial growth driven by technological advancements and increasing global healthcare infrastructure investment.
Major player in respiratory care devices market with strong positioning in PAP devices and home healthcare solutions, benefiting from market expansion and aging population trends.
Listed as a key company in the rapidly growing respiratory care devices market with ~8% CAGR through 2034, positioned to benefit from increasing demand for respiratory solutions.

title: 3 Reasons Not to Sleep on ResMed Stock
author: Selena Maranjian
description: ResMed, a leader in CPAP machines and respiratory care devices, presents an attractive investment opportunity with 11% year-over-year revenue growth, expanding digital health capabilities across 140+ countries, and a forward P/E ratio of 22 (below its five-year average of 29). However, investors should be aware of potential headwinds including uncertain Medicare reimbursement rates and emerging alternative treatments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: ResMed demonstrates solid fundamentals with 11% YoY revenue growth, rising gross profit margins, 18% operational income growth, and attractive valuation metrics (P/E of 22 vs. 5-year average of 29). Strong digital health ecosystem expansion and R&D investments support future growth, though the article acknowledges some risks like Medicare reimbursement uncertainty and competitive threats.
